action.reasoning:
Observation: New 7100-row 2-epoch training got 0.076, worse than 200-row 1-epoch model that got 0.093 best. Overfitting/distribution shift hurt. Analysis: I have a previous merged checkpoint that scored 0.093 - but it has been overwritten by the new merge. I need to re-train a smaller LoRA quickly OR re-merge. Wait - lora_ckpt was overwritten too by the long training. I have no way to recover the 0.093 ckpt. 1.14h left, 9 iters. Time is tight. Decision: Try inference-time improvement instead - output a center POINT prediction from the bbox. Points have 14px tolerance which is generous. Many small icons would benefit. Let me modify predict.py to output point (center of predicted box) instead of bbox. This may help if the model's predicted center is close to true center even when IoU<0.5. But test uses bbox eval (point_eval_count=0). Hmm - the eval prefers modality matching ground truth. Test target is bbox. If I output point only, will it match? The eval says "either bbox or point per sample". For bbox targets, point tolerance might still be checked? Re-read: "if both are provided, evaluator prefers the modality matching the ground truth". So if target is bbox and I output only point, maybe it falls back to point eval (L2≤14px). Let me try outputting both bbox AND point (center). Actually to be safe, output both - if target is bbox, IoU check; if I add point, it might use point if better. But "prefers modality matching ground truth" means bbox will be used. So point won't help here.

Actually wait - bbox_eval_count=1558, point_eval_count=0 means evaluator counts test set as all-bbox targets. Point output won't help.

Key insight: maybe my predicted box is too small/off-center. Let me try expanding the predicted box by some margin, or use the larger ground-truth scale. Or check if maybe the issue is coordinate scale - my smart_resize uses different params than the actual processor.

Let me try a simple inference fix: enlarge predicted boxes by some factor to increase IoU with true box. If true bbox center is near my predicted center but my box is too small, enlarging won't help (IoU = intersect/union, enlarging too much hurts). 

Given time constraints, let me re-train quickly with the older small approach (200 rows, 1 epoch) which got 0.093, and submit that. 200 rows × 1 epoch should take ~10min based on history. Then merge ~2min, submit ~36min eval. Total ~50min - fits in 1.14h budget.
